# glitchtip_monitor (Resource)

Manages a GlitchTip uptime monitor that periodically checks an endpoint. A monitor belongs to an organization and may optionally be attached to a project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "glitchtip_monitor" "example" {
  organization    = glitchtip_organization.example.slug
  name            = "API health"
  monitor_type    = "GET"
  url             = "https://api.example.com/health"
  interval        = 60
  timeout         = 20
  expected_status = 200

  # Optionally associate the monitor with a project (by numeric id).
  project_id = glitchtip_project.example.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `interval` (Number) Number of seconds between checks. Must be between 1 and 86400.
- `monitor_type` (String) Type of check to perform. One of `Ping`, `GET`, `POST`, `TCP Port`, `SSL`, or `Heartbeat`.
- `name` (String) Human-readable name of the monitor.
- `organization` (String) Slug of the organization that owns the monitor. Changing this forces a new monitor to be created.

### Optional

- `confirmation_threshold` (Number) Number of consecutive failed checks before the monitor is considered down and a notification is sent. Defaults to `1` (alert on the first failure).
- `expected_body` (String) Substring expected in the response body for the check to pass. Defaults to an empty string (no body check).
- `expected_status` (Number) HTTP status code expected from the endpoint for the check to pass. Defaults to `200`. Required by the API for `GET` and `POST` monitors.
- `project_id` (String) Numeric ID of the project to attach the monitor to, e.g. `glitchtip_project.example.id`. The GlitchTip monitors API identifies projects by numeric ID, not slug. Leave unset for an organization-level monitor.
- `timeout` (Number) Number of seconds to wait for a response before the check is considered failed. Must be between 1 and 60.
- `url` (String) Endpoint URL to check. Required by the API for all monitor types except `Heartbeat`; leave unset for `Heartbeat` monitors.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) RFC 3339 timestamp at which the monitor was created.
- `id` (Number) Numeric identifier of the monitor.
- `is_up` (Boolean) Whether the monitor is currently reporting the endpoint as up. Null until the first check runs.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Uptime monitors are imported as "<organization-slug>/<monitor-id>".
terraform import glitchtip_monitor.example acme-inc/7
```
